items.headline: EZB lässt Leitzins unverändert nach Zinserhöhung im Juni
items.summary: Die Europäische Zentralbank legt eine Zinspause ein. Der für Banken und Sparer wichtige Einlagensatz bleibt bei 2,25 Prozent. Gut möglich jedoch, dass im September die nächste Erhöhung folgt.

items.headline: Wie gerecht ist das Reformpaket der Koalition?
items.summary: Mehr Aufschwung, mehr Beschäftigung - mit ihrem Reformpaket will die Koalition das Land wieder fit machen. Nach der Sommerpause soll es verabschiedet werden. Doch wie gerecht sind die Gesetzentwürfe? Von Frank Aischmann.

items.headline: Batteriehersteller in der Krise: Varta soll zerschlagen werden
items.summary: Die Zukunft von Varta ist ungewiss. Beim angeschlagenen Batteriehersteller rückt eine Zerschlagung näher. Die wichtigsten Gläubiger wollen das Geschäft mit Haushaltsbatterien aus dem Konzern herauslösen.

items.headline: Verfassungsklage wegen Beratungszeit für "Heizungsgesetz" abgewiesen
items.summary: Der Zeitplan der damaligen Ampelkoalition für das Gebäudeenergiegesetz im Sommer 2023 war nicht zu knapp. Das hat das Bundesverfassungsgericht entschieden. Im Eilverfahren hatte der klagende CDU-Politiker noch Recht bekommen.

items.headline: Nach schweren Gefechten: Thailand baut Grenzzaun zu Kambodscha
items.summary: Nach den schweren Gefechten des vergangenen Jahres schafft Thailand Fakten: Entlang der Grenze zu Kambodscha steht der erste Abschnitt eines dauerhaften Grenzzauns. Das Nachbarland reagiert bislang zurückhaltend.

items.headline: UniCredit steht kurz vor Commerzbank-Übernahme
items.summary: Die italienische Großbank UniCredit steht kurz vor der Übernahme der Commerzbank. Bankchef Orcel sucht dazu jetzt das Gespräch mit allen Beteiligten. Willkommen ist er allerdings nicht.

items.headline: Wohnungsbau in Deutschland fällt auf 15-Jahrestief
items.summary: Trotz Wohnraummangels dürfte die Zahl der fertiggestellten Wohnungen in Deutschland weiter sinken. Das ifo Institut rechnet für 2026 mit nur noch 185.000 neuen Wohnungen. Erholung ist kaum in Sicht.

items.headline: Waldbrände in Frankreich und Spanien: Tausende in Sicherheit gebracht
items.summary: Im Südwesten Frankreichs sind mehrere Campingplätze und Ortsteile aufgrund von Waldbränden evakuiert worden. Auch in Spanien und Italien mussten Hunderte Menschen vor Bränden in Sicherheit gebracht werden.

items.headline: Google-Verstöße gegen DMA: EU verhängt insgesamt 890 Millionen Euro Strafe
items.summary: Seit Monaten untersucht Brüssel mögliche Verstöße von Google gegen den Digital Markets Act. Jetzt wurden Strafen über zusammen fast 900 Millionen Euro verhängt.

items.headline: Thunderbird: Neue ESR-Version mit Exchange-Support
items.summary: Thunderbird 153 „Meadow“ bringt einen neuen Einrichtungsdialog, native Exchange-Anbindung per EWS und mehr Funktionen in Benachrichtigungen.

items.headline: Astronomie: Erster plausibler Hinweis auf einen „supermerkwürdigen“ Exomond
items.summary: Mehrfach gab es Hinweise auf Monde in einem anderen Sternsystem, bestätigt wurde keiner. Die bislang besten Indizien weisen nun auf ein merkwürdiges Exemplar.

items.headline: KI-Modelle von OpenAI steckten hinter IT-Angriff auf Hugging Face
items.summary: Bei einem Test der Fähigkeiten neuer KI-Modelle sind die aus ihrer Umgebung ausgebrochen und haben Hugging Face kompromittiert. Das hat OpenAI eingestanden.

items.headline: Nextcloud: Wirrwarr um Website-Störung – Cyberangriff bestätigt
items.summary: Am Sonntag ging die Webseite von Nextcloud vorübergehend offline. Der Anbieter bestätigt auf Anfrage einen Cyberangriff.

items.headline: Suno-Datenleck: Have I Been Pwned ergänzt 55 Millionen Konten
items.summary: Das Have-I-Been-Pwned-Projekt hat mehr als 55 Millionen Konten aus dem Suno-Datenleck zur Datenhalde hinzugefügt.

items.headline: AliExpress: EU-Kommission verhängt DSA-Rekordstrafe
items.summary: Der chinesische Onlinemarktplatz AliExpress hat über Jahre gegen den Digital Services Act verstoßen und muss nun 550 Millionen Euro zahlen.

items.headline: Microsoft arbeitet an Lösung für WSUS-Störungen
items.summary: Die Update-Software WSUS hat seit vergangener Woche mit Problemen zu kämpfen, hat Microsoft nun eingeräumt. Es gibt erste Fixes.

items.headline: Anzeige: Strom auf Reisen: Winziger Reiseadapter von Anker zum neuen Bestpreis bei Amazon
items.summary: Wer im Urlaub keine bösen Überraschungen erleben will, braucht einen Reiseadapter. Das extrem kompakte Anker-Nano-Modell gibt es aktuell zum Bestpreis bei Amazon. (Technik/Hardware)

items.headline: Kommerzielle Raumfahrt: Russlands Recycling-Rakete kommt nicht vor 2030
items.summary: 2020 hatte Russland angekündigt, eine wiederverwendbare Rakete zu konstruieren, die SpaceX endlich Konkurrenz machen würde. Das dürfte aber dauern. (Roskosmos, SpaceX)

items.headline: Anzeige: Powerbank mit 22,5 W und 10.000 mAh für 18,29 Euro bei Amazon
items.summary: Amazon hat eine beliebte Powerbank von Iniu im Angebot, die im kompakten Format drei USB-Ports, 22,5 W und 10.000 mAh zur Verfügung stellt. (Technik/Hardware)

items.headline: Raumstation, Mondanzug & mehr: Die großen Pläne von Axiom für den Weltraum
items.summary: Mit der Axiom Station soll eine kommerzielle Raumstation im Erdorbit entstehen. Doch die Pläne des Unternehmens sind noch weitreichender. (Raumstation, Raumfahrt)

items.headline: Anzeige: Dieser Saugroboter von Roborock war noch nie günstiger
items.summary: Bei Amazon sind die beliebtesten Saugroboter von Roborock im Angebot. Wer die Wohnung mühelos reinigen möchte, kann jetzt günstig zuschlagen. (Saugroboter, Roborock)

items.headline: DMA: EU verhängt 890 Millionen Euro Strafe gegen Google
items.summary: Was steht oben, wenn Nutzer WM-Spiele, Hotels oder Routen googeln? Zu oft nur Googles eigene Angebote, urteilt Brüssel. (Google, Suchmaschine)

items.headline: Anzeige: USB-C-Kartenleser für Micro-SD-Karten jetzt für unter 9 Euro bei Amazon
items.summary: Laptops und Smartphones haben oft keinen Steckplatz mehr für Speicherkarten. Ein USB-C Kartenleser von Ugreen löst das Problem und kostet aktuell unter 9 Euro. (Technik/Hardware)

items.headline: (g+) Mehr Gehalt trotz Wirtschaftsflaute?: "Zum Üben Donald Trump spielen"
items.summary: Ist jetzt inmitten einer wirtschaftlichen Rezession der richtige Zeitpunkt für eine Gehaltsverhandlung? Ja, sagt unsere Expertin - und erklärt, warum und wie. Ein Interview von Peter Ilg (Gehalt, Wirtschaft)

items.headline: Saudis must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
items.summary: The US president says the landmark agreement to help Saudis develop nuclear energy is dependent on it joining the Abraham Accords.

items.headline: Trump's nuclear deal with Saudis jettisons longstanding US demands
items.summary: Past US presidents insisted that Saudi Arabia normalise relations with Israel in exchange for nuclear technology.

items.headline: 'I am still alive': Indian activist on hunger strike for 26 days loses 11kg
items.summary: Sonam Wangchuk has been the main draw at the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protest in Delhi.

items.headline: Liquor store employee narrowly escapes leopard attack
items.summary: After nearly five hours, officials captured the animal and it was later released back into the wild, local authorities confirmed.

items.headline: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
items.summary: Most of those evacuated were tourists from campsites around Arcachon Bay in France's Gironde region.

items.headline: 'Let's enjoy the ride' says Elon Musk, as AI fears mount
items.summary: Talking to The Economist, Musk said he doesn't care if people hate him and said he was not a racist.

items.headline: South Korea woman acquitted of murdering newborn after hospital delivery
items.summary: The woman, who had wanted to end her pregnancy at 36 weeks, gave birth to a baby who doctors later killed.

items.headline: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
items.summary: The company said earlier this year it expected to spend as much as $190bn on AI investments.

items.headline: Teenager drops social media addiction lawsuit against Meta
items.summary: Claims from a 15-year-old boy were set to go to trial next week in Los Angeles, but the case has now been dropped.

items.headline: OpenAI says its AI went rogue and launched 'unprecedented' cyber-attack
items.summary: It is one of the first publicly disclosed cyber-attacks carried out by AI without direct human involvement.

items.headline: Tech Life
items.summary: Dogs have a great sense of smell. Now machines are being trained to sniff out odours.

items.headline: AI minister role boosted but tech department axed in Burnham shake-up
items.summary: Kanishka Narayan will attend cabinet - but no longer have the leadership of a technology secretary.

items.headline: Thousands of websites taken down for illegal World Cup streams
items.summary: More than 1,000 illegal streaming sites were shut down in the US during the tournament, with a similar amount blocked in Colombia.

items.headline: Ofcom unable to take further legal action against suicide forum
items.summary: The regulator said it had "no further legal routes available" under the Online Safety Act.

items.headline: White House report says Trump can usher in a "new golden age" of science
items.summary: The resulting report is a near-random mixture of real problems and grievances.

items.headline: I wanted a clock that never needed setting. Things escalated.
items.summary: Sure, maybe your clock has a backup battery, but mine has a deployment pipeline!

items.headline: Orcas team up to ram sunfish until they explode
items.summary: “We think this may help younger orcas feed more easily, or it could also just be for fun.”

items.headline: Sales were up at Tesla but so were costs and spending
items.summary: Q2 2026 was profitable, but barely.

items.headline: Microsoft brings original Xbox backward compatibility to Windows PCs
items.summary: First four compatible titles can be run with an 11-year-old graphics card.

items.headline: ISPs' long nightmare of having to list all the fees they charge is finally over
items.summary: FCC lets ISPs stop listing all fees after companies complained it was too hard.

items.headline: Clayface trailer leans into the body horror
items.summary: "The people I trusted betrayed me. The justice system failed me."

items.headline: Hyundai claims humanoid robot plan is not part of talks with striking workers
items.summary: Union previously warned automaker that any robot deployment must be negotiated.

items.headline: Experts say exploiting Anthropic’s Fable isn’t how Kimi K3 got so good
items.summary: "I don't think you get a model this strong and this quickly on the heels of Fable doing strictly distillation," one expert told TechCrunch.

items.headline: ServiceNow bets $40 million on Indian banking software specialist to expand its financial services push
items.summary: ServiceNow's investment gives BusinessNext a strategic partner to expand its AI-powered banking software globally.

items.headline: After shocking quarter, IBM insists that AI isn’t killing the mainframe
items.summary: After IBM's stock crashed last week on warnings of poor mainframe sales, the CEO explained that AI wrecked corporate hardware budget, temporarily.

items.headline: Google justifies its massive AI spending with a booming cloud business
items.summary: Google's cloud business is thriving, as companies adopting its AI and AI infrastructure services help the tech giant to report record profits.

items.headline: Treasury threatens sanctions after White House claims Moonshot distilled Anthropic’s Fable
items.summary: The episode has also intensified a broader debate in Washington over the influx of Chinese open models.

items.headline: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face
items.summary: OpenAI made a mistake setting up what it called a “highly isolated” testing environment and sandbox. According to cybersecurity experts, that human mistake is what made the AI-powered attack on Hugging Face possible.

items.headline: Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
items.summary: Uber is also investing in Travis Kalanick's company Atoms, which has made gauzy claims about using industrial AI to modernize the world.

items.headline: Yope raises $12.3M to build a private social network without algorithms or ads
items.summary: Yope, a fast-growing social app focused on private groups of friends and family, has raised $12.3 million in seed funding. Instead of chasing creators and algorithmic feeds, the startup is betting that the future of social networking lies in small, private communities powered by

items.headline: AMD commits up to $5 billion to Anthropic
items.summary: AMD says it's going to invest up to $5 billion in Anthropic, while helping to expand the AI company's computing power, according to an announcement on Wednesday. As part of the new partnership, Anthropic will deploy up to 2 gigawatts of AMD's Instinct MI450 AI GPUs using the chip

items.headline: Meta made its own AI detection system. It should have just used Google’s
items.summary: Content Seal sounds a lot like a less accessible and reliable version of SynthID. | Image: Meta / The Verge IIn March, Meta's Oversight Board called on the company to "meet its public commitments and employ its own tools" to help quell the spread of deceptive generative AI conten

items.headline: Utility companies promise to spare us from AI’s energy bill
items.summary: The pledge was introduced in March, and has done little to quell concerns so far. | Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge, Getty Images In the face of backlash to concerns the AI boom will increase consumer electricity bills, the largest utility companies and data center developers in

items.headline: Neill Blomkamp’s new zombie AI ‘film’ is just slop warmed over
items.summary: On Monday, District 9 and Gran Turismo director Neill Blomkamp unveiled his latest project: a 13-minute sci-fi short titled Nightborne that's loosely based on Peter Watts' 2014 novel Echopraxia. The short comes from Blomkamp's new AI startup / production company, Barley Studios,

items.headline: OpenAI says it accidentally hacked Hugging Face with a new AI system
items.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman. | Bloomberg via Getty Images OpenAI says its AI models mistakenly breached open-source AI platform Hugging Face during internal testing. In a blog post on Tuesday, OpenAI writes that GPT-5.6 Sol and "an even more capable pre-release model" discovered vulner

items.headline: Substack adds an AI detector to help spot blogs written by no one
items.summary: Substack will now help users determine whether what they're reading may have been written by AI. A new tool coming to the platform can scan posts, notes, replies, and comments to provide an estimate of how much text could be AI-generated or written with AI assistance, according t

items.headline: The AI compute gap: Enterprises are buying infrastructure faster than they can measure what it costs
items.summary: Across 107 enterprises, AI infrastructure spending is accelerating well ahead of the ability to see or steer its economics. Most organizations run their AI on a familiar base of hyperscalers and model-provider APIs, yet the next dollar is aimed at specialized compute almost none

items.headline: The agent security gap: 54% of enterprises have already had an AI agent incident, and most still let agents share credentials
items.summary: Across 107 enterprises, AI agents are being given real access to systems and data while the controls meant to contain them lag behind. More than half have already had a confirmed agent security incident or a near-miss; only about a third give every agent its own scoped identity,

items.headline: The AI context gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a trust problem, not a retrieval problem — and most are still building the fix
items.summary: Across 101 enterprises, the infrastructure that feeds AI agents their business context is being built faster than it can be trusted. Retrieval-augmented generation is already the default context source, and provider-native retrieval has quietly overtaken the dedicated vector data

items.headline: The agent evaluation gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a reality-alignment problem, not a coverage problem — and most are shipping to production anyway
items.summary: Across 157 enterprises, organizations are granting AI agents more autonomy while trusting the evaluations meant to gate that autonomy less. Half have already shipped an agent that passed their internal evaluations and then failed a customer in production; only one in twenty fully

items.headline: Agentic orchestration: Enterprise AI organizations have a deployment problem, not a platform problem — and most are calling chatbots agents
items.summary: Across 101 enterprises, agent orchestration is consolidating onto model-provider platforms — Anthropic’s Claude leads by a wide margin — chosen for the gravity of the underlying model and judged on reliable multi-step execution. But the ambition runs well ahead of the reality: mo

items.headline: Google just redesigned the search box for the first time in 25 years — here’s why it matters more than you think.
items.summary: For a quarter century, the Google search box has been one of the most recognizable interfaces in computing: a thin white rectangle, a blinking cursor, a few typed words, and a list of blue links. On Tuesday, Google will formally retire that paradigm. At its annual I/O developer c

items.headline: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
items.summary: Railway, a San Francisco-based cloud platform that has quietly amassed two million developers without spending a dollar on marketing, announced Thursday that it raised $100 million in a Series B funding round, as surging demand for artificial intelligence applications exposes the

items.headline: How AI helps scientists design the next generation of medicines
items.summary: Designing and developing a new medicine is an expensive, failure-prone scientific challenge. A new drug can take many years to develop, at the cost of a significant investment. And even then, most possible candidates never reach the patient. For biologic medicines, therapies made

items.headline: Advancing next-gen AI with materials science innovation
items.summary: The conversation about AI often centers on algorithms, computing power, or huge investments in new semiconductor fabrication plants and hyperscale data centers. But beneath each of these advances is another layer of innovation that makes them possible: advanced materials. Every n

items.headline: AI is more likely than humans to form biases when hiring
items.summary: The next time you apply for a job, AI may screen your résumé before any human sees it. But there’s good reason to question whether AI will judge you fairly. Researchers already know that LLMs pick up human biases from their training data. New research suggests that LLMs can also

items.headline: The risk of weather data sabotage is rising
items.summary: Every morning, airline dispatchers, grid operators, and farmers around the world make decisions based on the same thing: a weather forecast. While these forecasts are something that most people glance at for two seconds, weather predictions influence major strategic decisions in

items.headline: Meet GPT-Red: an LLM super-hacker OpenAI built to make its models safer
items.summary: OpenAI has built an LLM super-hacker called GPT-Red that it uses as a sparring partner to help its other models boost their defenses against cyberattacks. Last week the company released the latest version of its flagship LLM, GPT-5.6. OpenAI says that training it against GPT-Red

items.headline: What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show

items.headline: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts
items.summary: The AI firm Anthropic has developed a technique that has given it the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on inside large language models as they answer questions or carry out tasks. What they found ranges from the mundane to the unnerving. Researchers at the company buil

items.headline: Remember Jibo? Its Successor Is a Wearable That Turns Your Life Into AI Slop
items.summary: With “blessings” from the original Jibo founders, iKairos is a wearable or desk-mounted “AI journal” that turns your family moments into AI images and video.

items.headline: The White House Is Trying to Figure Out What to Do About Chinese AI
items.summary: There’s a debate going on in the Trump administration over how to handle increasingly powerful Chinese AI models.

items.headline: China’s Open AI Models Are Challenging Silicon Valley’s Playbook
items.summary: As access to Anthropic’s and OpenAI’s frontier models becomes more restricted, Chinese labs are pitching their open-source alternatives as stable, accessible, and increasingly capable.

items.headline: OpenAI Models Escaped Containment and Hacked Hugging Face
items.summary: The cybersecurity-focused models, including GPT-5.6 Sol, broke out of a testing sandbox, exploited a zero-day, and gained access to the open internet to pull off the attack.

items.headline: This Former Intel CEO Wants to Jumpstart Moore’s Law With Light
items.summary: Pat Gelsinger wants to pave the way to ever more powerful artificial intelligence using tiny beams of light.

items.headline: A Sneaky Hacking Tool Targeting AI Infrastructure Is Lurking in Victims’ Blind Spots
items.summary: A new type of malware can worm deep into AI coding systems to steal data and logins—and can flip a “death switch” to destroy files and keep out real users.

items.headline: Nvidia Wants to Own Every Chip Inside AI Data Centers
items.summary: Nvidia’s Vera Rubin platform combines CPUs and GPUs into a single system, reflecting the company’s growing ambition to power every layer of AI infrastructure.

items.headline: Halliday’s New Smart Glasses Skip the Camera
items.summary: Halliday’s G2 glasses can listen to and summarize your workplace meetings—with no video involved.
